response.justification: The candidate is a strong polyglot engineer with genuine systems-level experience, but his profile does not match this role's core requirement of deep, current C++ expertise. His only professional C++ work was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list; his career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and DLang. He shows no exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL practices, MQTT, or the listed industrial protocols, which are central to this position.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic, handling millions of requests per second
Event-driven architecture and distributed-platform experience via GISCollective and microservices work
Strong mentoring, testing, and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, courses, TDD advocacy)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months professionally, ending in 2015); C++ is absent from his stated skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or OT-protocol experience
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not the networked multi-threaded C++ platform work this role demands

key_factors.factor: Solid distributed and concurrent systems fundamentals
